name: Ledger normalized event schema
purpose: Records from every source connector are normalized to this shape before entering the incident store. Each source-record stays linked to its origin; multiple source-records may resolve to a single incident.

record.source_record_id: string — the source's own ID for this record
record.source_url: string — direct URL to the source document or API record
record.ingested_at: ISO 8601 — when the connector wrote this record
record.country_iso: string — ISO 3166-1 alpha-3, or 'UNK' if disputed/non-state territory
record.secondary_locations: array of {lat, lng, name} — finer-grained where applicable
record.actor_raw: string — exact actor string from source
record.actor_normalized: string — canonical form, e.g. 'russia.state.military'; result of entity-resolution pass

casualties.dead_low: integer or null — when source publishes a range

record.source_bias_disclosure: string — when the source has known bias relevant to interpretation, e.g. State Dept reports do not cover US conduct
record.incident_id: string — the canonical incident this record resolves to
record.confidence: float 0-1 — derived from source count, source agreement, and corroboration window

incident_resolution.purpose: Collapse multiple source-records that describe the same event into one canonical incident, while preserving every source-record.
method: 1. Embed each record's location + actor + date + summary; nearest-neighbor candidate matching.
2. LLM verification pass on candidates: 'Are these the same incident? Yes / No / Probably / Different incidents same area.'
3. Produce canonical incident with: best-supported date, location, actor; min/max casualty range across sources; list of source-record ids.
4. If sources materially disagree (e.g. casualty figures differ by >50%), tier = 'disputed' and disagreement is preserved in the incident record.

changelog.purpose: Every change to a published record (correction, retraction, addition of new source) is logged with timestamp and reason. The changelog is public and queryable. This is the cultural design that separates a serious record from advocacy material.
